chapter_context.summary: Abijam's wicked reign in Judah is followed by Asa's righteous reign of reforms; Nadab reigns wickedly in Israel but is assassinated by Baasha, who exterminates Jeroboam's entire family.

outline.text: King Abijam's Wicked Reign in Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Abijam's Wicked Reign in Judah (15:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: King Asa's Righteous Reign in Judah; Banishes Idolatry
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Asa's Righteous Reign in Judah; Banishes Idolatry (15:9-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: War Between Asa and Baasha; Asa's Alliance with Ben-hadad
outline.explanation: This section focuses on War Between Asa and Baasha; Asa's Alliance with Ben-hadad (15:16-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: King Nadab's Wicked Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Baasha
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Nadab's Wicked Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Baasha (15:25-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Baasha's Wicked Reign in Israel Begins
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Baasha's Wicked Reign in Israel Begins (15:33-34), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 15, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
